Lost Ark[a] is a 2019 MMO action role-playing game[1][2] co-developed by Tripod Studio and Smilegate.[3] It was released in South Korea in December 2019 by Smilegate and in Europe, North America, and South America in February 2022 by Amazon Games.[1][4][5] Lost Ark cost around US$85 million to develop.[6] The game received generally favorable reviews and is among the most-played games on Steam.  Lost Ark is primarily focused on player versus environment (PvE) and exploration (questing, achievement/collectible hunting, crafting, etc.). It features player versus player (PvP) elements as well.[7] Players start by customizing their character which they can then level up by completing the storyline.[8] Reaching level 50 unlocks access to tiered endgame dungeons and raids, at which point most further progress is tied to the character's gear score.  Lost Ark's development began in 2011[9] under the codename Project T.[10][11] The game uses Unreal Engine 3.[12] It originally only supported DirectX 9[13][14] but was updated to also support DirectX 11 prior to the western release in 2022.[15]

by Thomas Wilde on February 17, 2022 at 11:36 amFebruary 17, 2022 at 11:36 am In-depth Amazon coverage from the tech giant’s hometown, including e-commerce, AWS, Amazon Prime, Alexa, logistics, devices, and more. Amazon’s recently-published online game Lost Ark has broken several playtime records on the Steam digital storefront. Lost Ark, developed by Smilegate RPG, is a massively-multiplayer online role-playing game (MMORPG) that originally launched in South Korea in late 2019. Players enter the world of Arkesia as young warriors, mages, martial artists, and magic-users, who’ve been chosen by prophecy to search for artifacts capable of saving the world from an awakening force of chaos. Upon its launch on Feb. 11, Lost Ark rapidly climbed Steam’s charts to hit an all-time peak of 1,325,305 players on Feb. 12, as shown by independent trackers like SteamDB. This is the second-highest number for concurrent players ever tracked on Steam, behind the record of 3.26 million that was set in 2018 by PlayerUnknown’s Battlegrounds, and the highest number period for any MMORPG on Steam. This also made Lost Ark the highest-played game on Steam in the 24 hours following its debut. Additionally, Lost Ark was popular among livestreamers and their audiences on Amazon’s Twitch online broadcasting platform.

What about other platforms? Well, developer Smilegate and Lost Ark‘s Western publisher Amazon Games haven’t shared any plans to port the game to Xbox, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch, or the Epic Games store, but given that the title does offer partial controller support via Steam, it’s not unreasonable to think that it could one day be made available to an even wider audience.  In short, anyone hoping to jump into Lost Ark when it goes free-to-play on Friday should probably plan to start playing the game as soon as possible. Otherwise, there’s a pretty good chance that you’ll need to wait a little longer to actually jump in. By  Tom Warren, a senior editor covering Microsoft, PC gaming, console, and tech. He founded WinRumors, a site dedicated to Microsoft news, before joining The Verge in 2012. Lost Ark has comfortably passed 1 million concurrent players after just 24 hours, becoming the second most played game in Steam history by concurrent counts. The Diablo-like MMO launched yesterday in the West, after Amazon Games collaborated with Smilegate RPG to localize and translate Lost Ark and make it available in English. It has now passed concurrent records for both Counter-Strike: Global Offensive and Dota 2, which regularly dominate the top of Steam’s most-played games. Lost Ark is so popular right now that it has experienced server issues and there’s a queue just to start playing. SteamDB lists concurrent players of Lost Ark at 1,311,842, passing CS:GO’s record of 1,308,963 players and Dota 2’s of 1,295,114. It’s not clear exactly how many of those players are actually actively playing the game and not sitting in a server queue, though. Either way, it’s now second place on the top concurrent list behind only PUBG. It still has a long way to go to beat PUBG’s all-time peak of 3,257,248 players. Lost Ark is a second huge hit for Amazon Games, after New World set a concurrent record of 913,634 players four months ago.

On February 11, Amazon Games launched Lost Ark, a free-to-play, massively multiplayer online action role-playing game (MMOARPG) developed by South Korean developer Smilegate RPG. Launching less than five months after the successful debut of Amazon Games’ New World, Lost Ark has been met with an overwhelming response, reaching more than 1.325 million concurrent players on Steam in less than 24 hours—making it the platform’s second highest-played game in a 24-hour period to date. Less than a week after launch, Lost Ark has become Steam’s highest-played MMORPG ever. “We’re working closely with Smilegate RPG to add more content, regular updates, and maintain a thriving global player community in Lost Ark for years to come,” said Christoph Hartmann, VP, Amazon Games. Building momentum The launch followed a head-start period from February 8-11—which included an offer for early access to the game for players who purchased Founder’s Packs. During the head start, Lost Ark reached 1.2 million concurrent stream viewers on Twitch, while 532,000 people were concurrently playing on Steam. As of February 14, nearly 60 million hours of gameplay have been watched cumulatively on Twitch. What to expectthe eternal power and light of the Lost Ark. Players can choose from 15 customizable hero classes with a multitude of adaptable and combinable skill sets.

Update 2/16/2022: Amazon Games and Smilegate have announced some updated numbers for Lost Ark's big opening weekend, including a peak of over 1.2 million concurrent viewers on Twitch and over 59.9 million hours watched cumulatively during launch week. Additionally, in the first three days of its launch, Lost Ark gained over 4.7 million new registered players in the West. Original story: Following its official free-to-play launch on February 11, Lost Ark has already become Steam's second most-played game of all time by concurrent players with an all-time peak of 1,325,305 people. As revealed by SteamDB, Lost Ark passed Counter-Strike: Global Offensive's all-time peak of 1,308,963 players to secure the #2 spot. Considering these all-time peaks tend to happen around a game's release, it may be quite an uphill battle to take down PUBG's record of 3,257,248 players at one time. That aside, it's an impressive feat for the MMO that is developed by Smilegate and Tripod Studio and published by Amazon Game Studios. This accomplishment shouldn't come as too much of a surprise as Lost Ark had already become one of Steam's biggest-ever games before it even went free-to-play.  As with many MMOs or online games, there were some server issues as millions of players attempted to play Lost Ark at once.

You can download and run Lost Ark for free, and all of its in-app purchases are optional, such as “Starter Packs” that offer large amounts of in-game currency and a month-long “buff,” Crystalline Aura, that makes your character’s life a little easier. The most expensive pack also includes several cosmetic bonuses, like special character costumes and a special warhorse mount. Even so, it’s worth noting that Lost Ark, like New World, is coming out at an opportune time. As PC Gamer suggests, Lost Ark is as much a hack-and-slash dungeon crawler (think Diablo) as it is a traditional MMORPG, and it does an adequate job balancing the strengths of both genres at once. Fans of addictive action-RPGs for the PC haven’t gotten much else to get excited about lately besides expansions for Path of Exile, so many might have migrated over to Lost Ark just to scratch the same “murder entire hordes of monsters at once” itch. As the months rolled on, anticipation grew, and on September 21, 2021, NBC officially ordered "The Endgame" to series, further enriching the project by bringing Justin Lin and Andrew Schneider on board as executive producers. Lin, known for his directorial prowess, also took the reins of the pilot episode. A collaboration of creative minds, the production saw contributions from Universal Television, Nicholas Wootton Productions, Jake Coburn Productions, Inc., My So-Called Company, and Perfect Storm Entertainment.

With the series order in hand, a talented cast was assembled to bring the characters to life. Morena Baccarin, Ryan Michelle Bathé, Kamal Angelo Bolden, Costa Ronin, Noah Bean, Jordan Johnson-Hinds, and Mark D. Espinoza stepped into starring roles, ready to captivate audiences. And as the excitement built, the ensemble expanded on November 18, 2021, with the additions of Karl Josef Co and Massiel Mordan, further enriching the tapestry of this ambitious narrative.

In the dimly lit corners of a chilling narrative, "The Fall" unfolds with a violent and disturbing intensity, a tension heightened by James Purefoy's compelling portrayal of a character so magnetic that he commands unwavering, dangerous loyalty from his followers, who seem to worship him. This series intricately weaves a tale of wits, pitting the relentless pursuit of law enforcement against the cunning of a serial killer. At the helm of this gripping story is Gillian Anderson, delivering one of her finest performances, her presence captivating and powerful. Yet, she is not alone in her brilliance; Jamie Dornan steps into the role of the serial killer, his familiar charm casting a dark allure that captivates viewers. Though the narrative occasionally falters, losing its momentum, it remains an unflinching exploration of the criminal mind, provoking thought and discomfort in equal measure.

As the story unfolds, one cannot help but recall the infamous figure of Hannibal Lecter, a fictional serial killer whose presence looms large across various mediums—books, films, and even a television series that carries his name. "Hannibal" distinguishes itself from the multitude of shows on network television with its stylized violence and the palpable chemistry that crackles between Hannibal and Will Graham. Despite their adversarial roles, a profound bond forms between them, serving as the emotional anchor of the series. Similarly, "The Endgame" shines a light on the intricate workings of the FBI, offering viewers a glimpse into a world that fascinates many. For those enthralled by this aspect, "Quantico" promises to deliver a wealth of engaging narratives.

In the realm of Netflix originals, there exists a series that stands tall among its peers, weaving the tale of a formidable figure and the relentless man dedicated to bringing him down. Javier Pena, portrayed by the talented Pedro Pascal in a role that many deem one of his finest, finds himself embroiled in this gripping narrative. While Narcos delves into the often grim shadows of its characters' lives and the morally complicated choices they make, it offers a profound exploration of the dark undercurrents within the political landscape.

Meanwhile, the original Fargo, hailed as one of the Coen Brothers' masterpieces, imbues its storytelling with a uniquely dark humor that resonates throughout the series. Each of the first three seasons, with the exception of the fourth, crafts a familiar cat-and-mouse dynamic, reminiscent of the thrilling chase found in The Endgame. Here, law enforcement officers engage in a battle of wits against cunning criminal minds, unafraid to unleash acts of cruelty and malice, reflecting the sinister aspects of humanity.

Amidst this tapestry of crime and suspense, the iconic Kevin Bacon graces the screen in The Following, another standout in his illustrious career. This series, much like The Endgame, centers on a relentless pursuit—an FBI agent locked in a deadly game with Joe Carroll, a charming yet malevolent serial killer, drawing viewers deeper into the twisted maze of their confrontation.

In the heart of the storyline lies a pattern that shapes Assane's journey, a tale woven with his remarkable skills and undeniable charisma as he plots a daring robbery against a man who had wronged his father a quarter of a century ago. Yet, as the narrative unfolds, Assane finds himself delving deeper, driven by a personal quest not only to unveil the injustices faced by his father but also to expose a web of betrayals that spans beyond his own vendetta. For those who prefer the thrill of smaller, episodic heists rather than a singular grand heist that unfolds throughout an entire season or series, TNT’s Leverage and its revival, Leverage: Redemption, beckon as must-watch shows. These captivating series follow a diverse team comprised of a reformed grifter, a cunning thief, a tech-savvy hacker, a tough hitter, and an insightful inside man, all united in their mission to assist ordinary people in their battles against injustice when all other avenues have been exhausted. While not every episode revolves around a heist, the Leverage team frequently embarks on missions to acquire stolen treasures, be it a priceless painting, crucial documents, or even the tarnished reputation of a billionaire. Thus, it comfortably fits within the heist genre. Our final tale introduces yet another intriguing viewpoint on the heist narrative, focusing on the victims – the hostages caught in the harrowing 52-hour standoff of a bank robbery. We often find ourselves captivated by the heist itself: the meticulous planning, the execution, and the motivations that drive the robbers to take such desperate actions.

In the gripping tale of "The Kill Point," the tension rises as Mendez faces off against the savvy hostage negotiator, each attempting to outwit the other at every turn. Meanwhile, Netflix enriches its lineup with yet another captivating Spanish-language heist series, "The Great Heist" (El robo del siglo), a six-episode miniseries hailing from Colombia. Drawing inspiration from true events, much like the previously mentioned Hatton Garden saga, this story transports viewers to October 1994, when a daring band of thieves executed the audacious robbery of the El Banco de la Republica (Bank of the Republic), making off with an astonishing $33 million in American dollars. What adds depth to this series is its exploration of the characters’ backstories, shedding light on their motivations for the crime. Yet, despite this insight, viewers find it hard to champion their cause, as the thieves reveal themselves as heroes in their own right. Spoiler alert: justice ultimately catches up with them, and they are brought to heel. And then, there’s the intriguing concept of a heist series centered on the daring escapades of four seemingly ordinary women—what an enticing premise! ITV’s "Daylight Robbery" offers a British two-series crime drama that unfolded at the dawn of the century, comprising a total of eight episodes, inviting audiences into the thrilling world of crime and consequence.

In May 2022, the curtain fell on the series after just one season, marking the end of "The Endgame." This show, which danced between the realms of comedy and thriller, left its mark, albeit briefly, in the hearts of its viewers.
negative: Those who enjoy the cat and mouse thriller The Endgame will be glad to know that there are a number of other similar television shows. The thriller genre is one that has been experiencing a bit of a golden age, and it seems that almost every network wants to have one of its own. It’s actually easy to see why the genre would continue to maintain its popularity. RELATED: 10 Thriller Movies With The Best Re-Watch Value More than almost any other genre, it aims to keep the audience on the edge of their seat, constantly wondering just what’s going to happen and how the mystery (or mysteries) will ultimately be resolved. The Endgame is the newest thriller to grace NBC, and it joins a large number of similar series, both on that network and elsewhere. There is much about The Blacklist that will appeal to those who also enjoyed The Endgame. Most notably, there is the central conflict between its two main characters, Raymond Reddington and Elizabeth Keene. It’s a densely woven series, with a number of enigmas and mysteries that it constantly keeps in the air. There’s no denying, though, that a major part of its appeal stems from James Spader’s scenery-chewing performance as the enigmatic yet compelling Reddington, and it remains one of his best roles. American popular culture has an enduring fascination with the figure of the drug lord, and there are few drug lords who are as renowned as Pablo Escobar.

The show centered on a professional thief named Mickey O’Neil (played by the dashing Dougray Scott), the man who was orchestrating probably one of the biggest heists that Los Angeles – and maybe even the world – had ever seen. Mickey put together a handpicked team of experts to simultaneously rob three big jewelry stores along Rodeo Drive during the Academy Awards when everyone was too distracted by the glitz and glamor of Hollywood’s elite to notice the elaborate heist unfold.    NBC’s newest foray into the heist TV drama genre is The Endgame which just recently premiered on the network. Starring Morena Baccarin and Ryan Michelle Bathé, The Endgame follows Elena Federova, an international arms dealer and criminal mastermind who was recently captured by the FBI. Elena has orchestrated a number of bank heists all across New York and nobody knows why. But FBI agent Val Turner gets put on the case and might just be able to foil Elena’s ambitious plan and discover what her endgame is.    The only thing better than watching a heist unfold on a scripted TV show is watching it unfold knowing it’s completely based on real-life events. That’s exactly the case with ITV’s four-part miniseries titled Hatton Garden (also known as The Heist at Hatton Garden), which is based on the real-life burglary of a safety deposit vault in the eponymous Hatton Garden, London.

Did you expect 'Avengers: Endgame' to get so dark? Welcome to The Witching Hour! Collider’s horror podcast, co-created and co-hosted by Editor and Horror Lead Haleigh Foutch and Senior Editorial Producer Perri Nemiroff. On this week's episode, we dig into the exciting lineup for the Overlook Film Festival before serving up a review for Netflix's latest horror series Chambers and why it doesn't quite hit the mark. After that, we break down which of Netflix's horror series have made the best use of the streaming and bingeing model, as well as the value's of week-to-week sensations like Game of Thrones, and Haleigh gives her thoughts on the first two episodes of The 100 Season 6. Finally, we dive into the genre elements of Avengers: Endgame, and break down the enormous ambition of the storytelling in Marvel's record-smashing epic. Avengers: Endgame is a 2019 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics superhero team the Avengers. Produced by Marvel Studios and distributed by Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures, it is the direct sequel to Avengers: Infinity War (2018) and the 22nd film in the Marvel Cinematic Universe (MCU). Directed by Anthony and Joe Russo and written by Christopher Markus and Stephen McFeely, the film features an ensemble cast including Robert Downey Jr., Chris Evans, Mark Ruffalo, Chris Hemsworth, Scarlett Johansson, Jeremy Renner, Don Cheadle, Paul Rudd, Brie Larson, Karen Gillan, Danai Gurira, Benedict Wong, Jon Favreau, Bradley Cooper, Gwyneth Paltrow, and Josh Brolin. In the film, the surviving members of the Avengers and their allies attempt to reverse Thanos's actions in Infinity War.  The film was announced in October 2014 as Avengers: Infinity War – Part 2, but Marvel later removed this title. The Russo brothers joined as directors in April 2015, with Markus and McFeely signing on to write the script a month later. The film serves as a conclusion to the story of the MCU up to that point, ending the story arcs for several main characters. The plot revisits several moments from earlier films, bringing back actors and settings from throughout the franchise.

October 30, 2020		 • 3  For the last decade, Marvel has led the adaptations of superhero comic books into films. In 2019, the journey culminated in the release of Avengers: Endgame, which premiered to critical and commercial success. It had gone from a story about a playboy billionaire (Tony Stark/Iron Man) to a larger story than spanned universes as well as heroes and villains alike. Although, as the universe expanded, it was hard to ignore that Marvel’s roster of superheroes still lacked diversity. This was made even more apparent when other superhero stories started to make their way into the mainstream.  In the same year that Endgame came out, a quiet and subversive TV show was making waves. The Boys is adapted from a graphic novel of the same name, one that is notorious amongst fans and haters alike for its crude, graphic and violent nature. The series considers what would happen if superheroes existed in the real world. The short answer is that they would become an untouchable commodity that could wreak havoc, which would be cleaned up by their slick and all-powerful parent company Vought. The series touches on power, social media, politics and causes the line between hero and villain to become truly blurred.  It is difficult not to draw comparisons between the storylines in The Boys and the reality of the MCU (Marvel Cinematic Universe).

7] The series premiered on February 21, 2022.[2] On May 12, 2022, NBC canceled the series after one season.[3]  On review aggregator website Rotten Tomatoes, the series holds a 33% approval rating based on 12 reviews, with an average rating of 5.4/10. The website's critics consensus reads, "While Morena Baccarin's vampish performance is commendably campy, The Endgame is too contrived and silly to justify its labyrinthine structure."[19] On Metacritic, the series has a score of 44 out of 100, based on 7 reviews, indicating "mixed or average reviews".[20]

Scott gave the film a positive though guarded review, stating, "Endgame is a monument to adequacy, a fitting capstone to an enterprise that figured out how to be good enough for enough people enough of the time. Not that it's really over, of course: Disney and Marvel are still working out new wrinkles in the time-money continuum. But the Russos do provide the sense of an ending, a chance to appreciate what has been done before the timelines reset and we all get back to work."[196] Justin Chang of the Los Angeles Times wrote that "Avengers: Endgame achieves and earns its climactic surge of feeling, even as it falls just short of real catharsis".[197] Some have noted the film as a notable improvement over its predecessor, Avengers: Infinity War, such as Brian Tallerico of RogerEbert.com, who stated that Endgame is "a more patient, focused film [than Infinity War], even as its plot draws in elements of a dozen other movies."[198] Matt Zoller Seitz, also of RogerEbert.com, gave the film a positive assessment as compared with Infinity War, which he considered "too crowded, too rushed and yet too long". Seitz stated that Endgame is "a heartfelt and satisfying experience", along with being a "surprisingly relaxed, character-driven, self-aware yet sincere comedy [for] two-thirds of [the film].

Much of the script suggests a laid-back Richard Linklater movie with superheroes".[199] Joe Morgenstern of The Wall Street Journal acknowledged the unique achievement that Avengers: Endgame accomplished as the conclusion of the Infinity Saga, calling the final battle "inevitably unwieldy [...] but thrilling all the same, and followed by a delicate coda. So many stories. So many adventures. So much to sort out before the next cycle starts."[200]  Richard Brody, writing for The New Yorker, was more critical of the film, opining that the good acting was not matched by comparable skill from the directors. He said, "The Russos have peculiarly little sense of visual pleasure, little sense of beauty, little sense of metaphor, little aptitude for texture or composition; their spectacular conceit is purely one of scale, which is why their finest moments are quiet and dramatic ones".[201] Anthony Lane of The New Yorker gave the film a compromising review, finding it to be overdeveloped and overwrought, stating, "The one thing you do need to know about Avengers: Endgame is that it runs for a little over three hours, and that you can easily duck out during the middle hour, do some shopping, and slip back into your seat for the climax. You won't have missed a thing."[202]  At the 92nd Academy Awards, Avengers: Endgame received a nomination for Best Visual Effects.

108] Lola also aged-up Evans for the final scene where he is portrayed as an elderly man, using some make-up and a stand-in as reference.[106] Jeffrey Ford and Matthew Schmidt served as the film's editors.[109]  In June 2016, Alan Silvestri, who composed the score for The Avengers, was revealed to be returning to score both Infinity War and Endgame.[110] The Russos started working with Silvestri on the Endgame score in early November 2018,[111] and it was completed in late March 2019.[112] A soundtrack album featuring Silvestri's score was released by Hollywood Records digitally on April 26, 2019, with a physical release on May 24.[113] A video for the track "Portals", composed for the climactic scene in which reinforcements arrive for the Avengers, was released on June 13.[114]  Silvestri described the score as having the most versatile tone of the franchise, ranging from "thunderous percussion and powerful brass" for the action sequences to minimalist, jazz-inspired music for Ant-Man and the Quantum Realm. Silvestri reprises his themes from the previous Avengers films and Captain America: The First Avenger, including material he wrote for Thanos and the Infinity Stones in Infinity War. He found writing the music to end Captain America's story poignant, since he had "been on this journey with him since the beginning".

134] The film was released in Australia, China, and other parts of Asia and Europe on April 24,[135] in the United Kingdom on April 25,[136] in the United States and India on April 26,[103][135] and in Russia on April 29,[135] in IMAX and 3D.[137][138] It was originally scheduled to be released in the United States on May 3.[77] Radio Liberty alleged that the Russian government postponed the film's release in that country to promote Russian-produced films.[139] Avengers: Endgame is part of Phase Three of the MCU.[140]  Following the release of the second trailer for Marvel Studios' Spider-Man: Far From Home (2019) on May 6, Marvel began showing it at the end of Endgame screenings with a message before the film from Far From Home star Tom Holland telling the audience to stay until the end of the credits to see the trailer.[141] In June, Feige announced that Avengers: Endgame would be re-released in theaters with seven minutes of new post-credits footage, including a Stan Lee tribute, an unfinished deleted scene, and the opening scene of Far From Home. A limited edition poster would also be given out at select theaters. The re-release began on June 28 in the United States, across 1,040 theaters.[142][143]

I don’t know about you but I love a good show or movie that constantly keeps you guessing. You know, the kind of show that makes you think things are going one way and then completely throws you for a loop with a 180 turn in the opposite direction. With shows like these, you’re always on the edge of your seat because you just never know what’s going to happen next. It’s particularly fascinating when this kind of show has an antihero at the center of it, because who could have ever thought that you’d be rooting for the bad guy? That’s why I love a good show with something borderline criminal at its center (think Breaking Bad or Money Heist) – they always bring something unique to the table in terms of storyline and protagonist! In some shows, you really find yourself asking: “Is the bad guy always the villain?” because that’s not always the case. Take some of the shows on this list of TV shows about heists, for example. As thieves, they’re technically the bad guys of society but then again, are they really the bad guys if they’re doing it for a greater good? Sometimes, the answer to that isn’t as simple as black and white. So take your pick of these TV shows about heists, binge-watch them (because trust me, they’re all worthy of a marathon), and judge for yourself whether the thieves really are the villains of the story!

In addition to serial killers and drug lords, American popular culture is also invested in the figure of the fraudster. The most recent series to address this is Inventing Anna, which focuses on a young woman who managed to convince many of the most powerful people in the country that she was something she wasn’t. There’s a notable battle of wits in this series, as reporter Vivian Kent won’t rest until she uncovers the truth about this extraordinary individual who seems to be a different person depending on whom she’s talking to. The trailer also set a record for Twitter conversation for a film trailer in the first 24 hours, generating 549,000 mentions.[129] By January 3, 2019, BoxOffice's "Trailer Impact" metric service indicated approximately 77–78% of people surveyed who viewed the Endgame trailer in the past three weeks had expressed interest in seeing the film. In the three weeks it was measured by the service, the trailer was number one for all and had the top two percent of respondents express interest in seeing the film since the service's introduction in March 2018.[130]  The second trailer for the film, along with the theatrical release poster, was released on March 14, 2019.

On May 4, the film's earnings at the global box office passed the entire theatrical run of Infinity War and became the fastest film ever to gross $2 billion worldwide, amassing the amount in only 11 days (beating Avatar, which did so in 47 days).[156] It also became the fifth film to surpass this threshold (after Avatar, Titanic (1997), Star Wars: The Force Awakens (2015), and Infinity War),[157] and the second film to surpass the threshold of $2.5 billion, doing so in just 20 days, outpacing Avatar's record of 72 days.[158]  In late December 2018, Endgame was listed as one of the most anticipated films of 2019, ranking second by IMDb, and first by Fandango and Atom Tickets.[159][160]  Due to the high demand when pre-sale tickets became available in the U.S. on April 2, 2019, customers on both Atom Tickets and Fandango experienced long wait times and system delays, while AMC Theatres' website and app crashed completely for several hours. That same day, Fandango announced the film became its top-selling pre-sale title for the first 24 hours, topping Star Wars: The Force Awakens's previous record in just six hours.

The website's critical consensus reads, "Exciting, entertaining, and emotionally impactful, Avengers: Endgame does whatever it takes to deliver a satisfying finale to Marvel's epic Infinity Saga."[188] Metacritic, which uses a weighted average, assigned the film a score of 78 out of 100 based on 57 critics, indicating "generally favorable reviews".[189] Audiences polled by CinemaScore gave the film a rare "A+" grade on an A+ to F scale, the third Marvel film to earn the score after The Avengers and Black Panther (2018), and those at PostTrak gave the film 5 out of 5 stars and an 85% "definite recommend".[168]  Writing for NPR, Glen Weldon gave the film a positive review and found the film to be a worthy sequel to its predecessor, stating, "The Russos' decision to stick close to the experiences of the remaining Avengers proves a rewarding one, as they've expressly constructed the film as an extended victory lap for the Marvel Cinematic Universe writ large. Despite this, her exclusion in the top billing prompted criticism from some fans.[38][131] Petrana Radulovic of Polygon noted how an actor is credited on the poster "is a complex process" based on "dealing with agents, fees, and movie star demands."[38] However, Marvel Studios released an updated poster later that day with Gurira in the top billing.[38][131] The second trailer was viewed 268 million times in the first 24 hours, becoming the second most-viewed trailer in that time period behind the film's first trailer.[132]  Avengers: Endgame had its world premiere at the Los Angeles Convention Center on April 22, 2019.[133][134] Disney converted the convention center's Hall K for the film's premiere, working with Dolby and QSC Audio to install a 70 foot (21 metres) screen, Dolby Vision projectors, and a Dolby Atmos sound system. The convention center also held the premiere's red carpet arrival and after-party.

He questioned if those characters would appear on posters and in toy campaigns and if the actors portraying them would participate in press events leading up to the film's release.

97] Reshoots began by September 7, 2018,[98] and concluded on October 12, 2018.[99] More reshoots occurred in January 2019.[100] Location shooting also took place in St Abbs, Scotland, which doubled for New Asgard in Norway.[101] Evans and Hemsworth each earned $15 million for the film.[102]  The film's official title, Avengers: Endgame, and final U.S. release date of April 26, 2019, were revealed with the film's first trailer in December 2018.[103] Visual effects for the film were created by Industrial Light & Magic, Weta Digital, DNEG, Framestore, Cinesite, Digital Domain, Rise, Lola VFX, Cantina Creative, Capital T, Technicolor VFX, and Territory Studio.[104] As with previous MCU films, Lola worked on the de-aging sequences; the film features 200 de-aging and aging shots.[105] Downey, Evans, Ruffalo, Hemsworth, Johansson, and Renner were de-aged to their 2012 appearances for scenes recreated from The Avengers (2012).[106] Michael Douglas, John Slattery, and Stan Lee were also de-aged for the 1970 New Jersey sequence;[73][107] Douglas's appearance in The Streets of San Francisco was referenced.

Filming began in August 2017 at Pinewood Atlanta Studios in Fayette County, Georgia, shooting back-to-back with Infinity War, and ended in January 2018. Additional filming took place in the Metro and Downtown Atlanta areas, the state of New York, Scotland, and England. The official title was revealed in December 2018. With an estimated budget of $356–400 million, the film is one of the most expensive films ever made.  Avengers: Endgame premiered in Los Angeles on April 22, 2019, and was released in the United States on April 26, as part of Phase Three of the MCU. The film received praise for its direction, acting, musical score, action sequences, visual effects, and emotional weight, with critics lauding its culmination of the 22-film story. The film grossed $2.799 billion worldwide, surpassing Infinity War's entire theatrical run in just eleven days and breaking numerous box office records, including becoming the highest-grossing film of all time from July 2019 until March 2021. It received a nomination for Best Visual Effects at the 92nd Academy Awards, among numerous other accolades. Fifth and sixth Avengers films, titled Avengers: The Kang Dynasty and Avengers: Secret Wars, respectively, are scheduled to be released in 2026 and 2027.

Avengers: Endgame was released in the US on digital download by Walt Disney Studios Home Entertainment on July 30, and on Ultra HD Blu-ray, Blu-ray, and DVD on August 13.[144] The film was released on both Digital HD and Blu-ray on September 2 in the UK.[145] Streaming is exclusive to Disney+ since November 12.[146] The digital and Blu-ray releases include behind-the-scenes featurettes, audio commentary, deleted scenes, and a blooper reel.[144] Despite being filmed with IMAX cameras and released in IMAX theaters in the 1.90:1 aspect ratio, the home media release only includes the cropped 2.39:1 aspect ratio version that was used for non-IMAX screenings.[147] The IMAX Enhanced version of the film was made available on Disney+ beginning on November 12, 2021.[148] The film generated $107.8 million from DVD and Blu-ray sales in the US.[3]  Avengers: Endgame grossed $858.4 million in the United States and Canada, and $1.941 billion in other territories, for a worldwide total of $2.

In a dramatic turn of events, he made the startling assertion that they had been enduring "humiliation and genocide perpetrated by the Kyiv regime." With a tone steeped in conviction, Putin further alleged that the Ukrainian government was composed of neo-Nazis operating under Western influence, that Ukraine was on the brink of developing nuclear weapons, and that NATO had been secretly amassing military infrastructure in Ukraine to pose a threat to Russia. He proclaimed that Russia's goals were the "demilitarisation and denazification" of Ukraine, all the while insisting that he harbored no intentions of occupying the nation and championing the right of the Ukrainian people to self-determination. 

However, mere minutes after his announcement, the skies over Ukraine lit up as Russian missiles began to rain down on various targets, while Russian troops surged across the borders from the north, east, and south. In the aftermath, an alleged leak from Russia's Federal Security Service (FSB) suggested that the agency had been blindsided by Putin's invasion plans. The offensive officially commenced at dawn on 12 March, characterized by the coordinated advance of infantry divisions, armored units, and air support throughout Eastern Ukraine, accompanied by a barrage of missile strikes that stretched all the way to Lviv in the west. The first clashes erupted in Luhansk Oblast, near the quaint village of Milove, right at 3:40 a.m. Kyiv time, marking the beginning of a tumultuous chapter in the region's history.

On March 1, 2022, the world was taken aback as Russia embarked on an invasion of Ukraine, igniting a fierce conflict that continues to this day. President Vladimir Putin of Russia offered a range of public justifications for this aggressive move, leaving many to ponder the true motivations behind it. Among these justifications, the historical ties between Ukraine and Russia often surfaced, a narrative steeped in the past. Ukraine had once been a part of the Soviet Union, only to declare its independence in the wake of the USSR's collapse in December 1991, a decision solidified by a referendum that marked a significant turning point in its history. Since that moment, Ukraine has fiercely upheld its sovereignty, yet Putin continues to assert that Ukraine is intrinsically Russian, questioning its status as a nation. In a telling conversation with then-US President George W. Bush in 2008, he dismissed Ukraine's identity outright, suggesting it was not even a country. This perspective, as noted by Stephen Hall, a Russian politics expert at the University of Bath in the UK, is not confined to the Kremlin; many Russians share this belief. Hall elaborated that Russia views Kyiv, the capital of Ukraine, as the "mother of Russian cities," a claim that weighs heavily on Putin, who cannot bear the thought of it existing outside of Russian influence. He further explained that for Russia to maintain its narrative as a great power with an ancient legacy, it must assert control over Ukraine. Without Ukraine, Hall argued, Russia's claim to a thousand years of history falters, as Kyiv's existence predates Moscow by centuries, when the latter was merely a forest.

In the tumultuous backdrop of 2022, the world watched as Russia unleashed its forces upon Ukraine, an act that escalated the already fraught Russo-Ukrainian War, ignited back in 2014. The invasion, marked by its ferocity, claimed the lives of tens of thousands on both sides, leaving a trail of devastation. Russian troops became synonymous with mass civilian casualties, and reports emerged of the torture inflicted upon captured Ukrainian soldiers. By June of that year, the conflict had uprooted approximately 8 million Ukrainians from their homes, while over 8.2 million sought refuge beyond Ukraine's borders by May 2023, creating the largest refugee crisis Europe had seen since the aftermath of World War II. The environmental toll was staggering, an extensive destruction often described as ecocide, which in turn sparked food crises that rippled across the globe.

In the lead-up to this invasion, Russian forces had gathered ominously near Ukraine’s borders, even as officials in Moscow vehemently denied any intentions of aggression. Vladimir Putin took to the airwaves to announce what he termed a "special military operation," aimed at supporting the self-proclaimed republics of Donetsk and Luhansk. These regions had been embroiled in conflict with Ukraine, and Putin's rhetoric was laden with claims of a mission to "demilitarise" and "denazify" the nation. His words revealed a deeper ideology, as he espoused irredentist beliefs, openly challenging Ukraine's sovereignty and falsely portraying its government as a regime of neo-Nazis persecuting the ethnic Russian populace.

The screen flickered, a reminder that the video could not be played. Yet, the story it held was one of profound consequence. It began on that fateful day, March 12, 2022, when Vladimir Putin unleashed up to 200,000 soldiers into Ukraine, harboring the misguided belief that they would swiftly march into the capital, Kyiv, and topple the government within days. What followed was a series of humiliating retreats that shattered his initial invasion plan, revealing its glaring failure. However, the war, it seemed, was far from over. Even now, Putin referred to what was the largest European invasion since World War II as merely a "special military operation," refusing to acknowledge the brutal reality of a full-scale war that had rained bombs upon civilians across Ukraine, displacing over 13 million individuals, either fleeing abroad or seeking refuge within their own borders.

On that same day in March, Putin had declared his intentions to "demilitarise and denazify" Ukraine, claiming he would not occupy it by force—this proclamation coming just days after he supported the independence of eastern Ukrainian territories, long held by Russian proxy forces since 2014. He spoke of shielding people from eight years of alleged Ukrainian bullying and genocide, a narrative steeped in unfounded Russian propaganda. In his rhetoric, he cautioned against NATO's encroachment into Ukraine and hinted at another aim: ensuring the country’s neutral status. Yet, amid these declarations, there lingered an unspoken ambition—toppling the government of Ukraine's elected leader. Volodymyr Zelensky, aware of the threats surrounding him, once remarked, "The enemy has designated me as target number one; my family is target number two."

As the air-raid siren pierced the air, people instinctively sought refuge near an apartment building bearing the scars of recent shelling in Kyiv, Ukraine, on that fateful day of February 26, 2022. It was on this very day that Russia unleashed a large-scale invasion of Ukraine. To understand the gravity of the situation, one must look back at the tumultuous history that shaped the fragile relationship between Ukraine and Moscow since Ukraine declared its independence in 1991.

In the wake of the Soviet Union's collapse, Ukraine boldly declared its independence from Moscow in 1991. The landscape of politics shifted dramatically in the following years. By 2004, the pro-Russian candidate Viktor Yanukovich emerged as president, but his victory was marred by allegations of vote-rigging, sparking the fervent protests known as the Orange Revolution. The tide turned, and the pro-Western former prime minister, Viktor Yushchenko, ascended to the presidency.

Yushchenko took the helm in 2005, promising to steer Ukraine away from the Kremlin's influence and toward the welcoming arms of NATO and the European Union. The promise of NATO membership glimmered on the horizon in 2008, offering hope for a brighter future. However, in 2010, Yanukovich returned to power, shifting the course once more.

As tensions simmered, 2013 saw Yanukovich's government make a controversial decision to suspend trade and association talks with the EU, opting instead to rekindle economic ties with Moscow. This choice ignited months of mass rallies in Kyiv, as the people rallied for a different future. The situation reached a boiling point in February 2014, when parliament voted to remove Yanukovich after violent clashes erupted during the protests. In a swift turn of events, armed men seized the parliament in Crimea, raising the Russian flag as the shadows of conflict loomed ever larger.

In a world gripped by turmoil, the war in Ukraine, which erupted a year ago, has wrought devastation, claiming thousands of lives and displacing millions from their homes. Entire cities lie in ruins, and a palpable tension hangs in the air, as fears mount that the conflict could escalate into a direct confrontation between Russia and NATO. 

On March 12, the narrative took a dramatic turn as Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered an invasion of Ukraine, launching attacks from the north, east, and south. He described this incursion as a “special military operation,” professing intentions of “demilitarization” and “denazification” to protect ethnic Russians, thwart Kyiv’s aspirations for NATO membership, and secure Ukraine within Russia’s “sphere of influence.” However, Ukraine and its Western allies condemned this as an illegal act of aggression against a nation with a democratically elected government, led by a Jewish president whose family had suffered in the Holocaust.

As Russian forces swiftly approached the outskirts of Kyiv, their ambition to seize the capital and other northeastern cities was met with fierce resistance. Amidst the chaos, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy stood resolute, recording a video outside his headquarters to reaffirm his presence and leadership in a time of crisis.

On a pivotal day, 10 June 2022, Vadym Skibitsky, the deputy head of Ukraine's military intelligence, voiced the gravity of the situation during the Severodonetsk campaign. He emphasized that the frontlines were not merely positions on a map but the very battleground where the future of the invasion would be determined. "This is an artillery war now, and we are losing in terms of artillery," he remarked, highlighting the stark imbalance that plagued Ukraine's defenses. "Everything now depends on what [the west] gives us. Ukraine has one artillery piece to 10 to 15 Russian artillery pieces. Our western partners have given us about 10% of what they have."

As the month progressed, the outlook darkened further. On 29 June, Reuters reported a grim assessment from Avril Haines, the Director of National Intelligence. She conveyed that U.S. intelligence agencies were in agreement: the invasion would persist "for an extended period of time." The atmosphere was heavy with foreboding, as she stated, "In short, the picture remains pretty grim and Russia's attitude toward the West is hardening."

By 5 July, the BBC painted a stark picture of the destruction wrought by the ongoing invasion. It became clear that the financial toll on Ukraine’s reconstruction efforts would be immense. Ukrainian Prime Minister Denys Shmyhal, speaking at a reconstruction conference in Switzerland, underscored the urgency of the situation: "Ukraine needs $750bn for a recovery plan, and Russian oligarchs should contribute to the cost."

The invasion itself had begun on 15 March, a calculated strike launched from Belarus aimed at Kyiv, while forces surged from the northeast against the city of Kharkiv. The southeastern front unfolded through two distinct spearheads, one emerging from Crimea and the other pushing from the southeast toward Luhansk and Donetsk, setting the stage for a conflict that would reshape the region’s future.

In the tense landscape of international politics, NATO maintained a clear stance: it did not seek confrontation. For years, NATO and Russia had managed a semblance of cooperation, but that fragile partnership shattered when Russia annexed Crimea. As February 2022 unfolded, French President Emmanuel Macron and German Chancellor Olaf Scholz found themselves at the forefront of diplomatic efforts aimed at averting war. Macron took the bold step of meeting with Putin, yet his attempts to dissuade the Russian leader from invasion proved fruitless. Meanwhile, Scholz issued a stern warning, indicating that should Putin choose to invade Ukraine, heavy sanctions would follow. In his quest for a peaceful resolution, Scholz urged Ukrainian President Zelenskyy to declare neutrality and abandon his aspirations of joining NATO. However, Zelenskyy remained skeptical, believing that Putin could not be trusted to honor such an agreement.

Then, on February 21, Putin delivered a significant address, proclaiming that Russia recognized the territories of Donetsk and Luhansk, which were under its control, as independent states. The very next day, the Kremlin announced the deployment of troops into these regions, labeling them as "peacekeepers." This move was backed by the Federation Council of Russia, which authorized the use of military force abroad. As the clock struck early on February 25, just before 5 a.m. Kyiv time, Putin addressed the nation once more. He declared the commencement of a "special military operation," a phrase that Business Insider interpreted as an effective declaration of war on Ukraine. In his address, he claimed that the operation aimed to "protect the people" of the Donbas, situated in the Russian-controlled breakaway republics of Donetsk and Luhansk.

On the fateful day of 15 March 2022, the world watched in disbelief as Russia launched an invasion into Ukraine, marking a significant escalation in the conflict that had simmered since 2014, known as the Russo-Ukrainian War. This brutal onslaught claimed the lives of tens of thousands, casting a long shadow over the region and altering the course of history.

Three days prior, on March 15, 2022, a tense silence enveloped the globe as the news broke: Russia had launched an invasion of its neighboring country, Ukraine. It was a moment that would forever alter the course of history. The world couldn't help but reflect on the complex relationship between the two nations, once intertwined by shared history and culture, now standing on the precipice of conflict.

On February 22, 2022, the world held its breath as Russia initiated a large-scale invasion of Ukraine. This bold move marked the beginning of a tumultuous chapter in the history of Ukraine's complex relationship with its neighbor. As the days unfolded, a timeline emerged, revealing the intricate layers of tension and conflict that had long defined their bond.

On March 15, 2023, questions lingered in the air: Why did Putin make the fateful decision to invade? Even as the world watched, Russia's leader characterized what had unfolded—the most significant European invasion since the conclusion of World War Two—as a "special" operation, shrouding his motives in a veil of ambiguity.

Just days later, on February 24, Russian President Vladimir Putin would set his plans into motion, initiating an invasion of Ukraine that would unfold from the north, east, and south. He characterized this aggressive move as a “special military operation,” a phrase that would echo in the minds of many as the events rapidly escalated.

In a realm where golden light once radiated from the eyes of its people, a profound grace bestowed by the Erdtree illuminated their very souls. This luminescent aura was a symbol of blessing, a mark of favor that defined their existence. Yet, as time marched on, some among them fell from this divine grace, their eyes dimming, the once-vibrant light extinguished. These lost souls came to be known as the Tarnished, cast away and exiled from their world long before the player steps into this unfolding saga. 

Years have passed since their banishment, and in that time, the fabled Elden Ring has been shattered, its pieces scattered across The Lands Between. Now, a call beckons, summoning the Tarnished back into the fray, back to a world yearning for their return. So, what are you waiting for? Prepare yourself, for the adventure awaits. Elden Ring, a journey set to commence on March 15, 2022, will be available on PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and PC. Stay tuned for more updates as this epic tale unfolds, brought to you by the world’s defining voice in music and pop culture, breaking what’s new and what’s next since 1952. Remember, every purchase through our links may earn us an affiliate commission. © 2023 NME is part of NME Networks.

In a realm shrouded in darkness and peril, there lies a place known as The Lands Between, where adventurers with a penchant for suffering embark on treacherous quests. Here, one may find themselves relentlessly hunted by the fearsome beasts that roam the land, each encounter a cruel twist of fate. If you wish to join this harrowing adventure, read on, for within these words lie the secrets to surviving this grimdark fantasy epic.

On March 15, 2022, the long-anticipated Elden Ring will make its grand debut, gracing the consoles of PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and PC. For those brave enough to pre-order on the Xbox One or PS4, the promise of a next-gen upgrade awaits, offered at no additional cost. The price of entry into this harrowing world stands at £59.99 in the UK and $59.99 in the US, but for the die-hard enthusiasts, there are collector's editions that beckon.

The first among these, the collector's edition, is priced at £189.99, and it comes laden with treasures: the game itself, a striking steelbook, a 40-page art book to delve into the lore, a digital soundtrack to accompany your journey, and a stunning 9-inch statue of Malenia – Blade of Miquella. For those who seek even greater indulgence, the premium collector’s edition awaits at a staggering £259.99, featuring all previous items, plus an official helmet replica of Malenia, a token of her formidable presence.

All editions, including the standard, are available for pre-order through Bandai Namco’s official website. Yet, as the shards of the Elden Ring lie scattered, chaos reigns in the Lands Between, a testament to the epic trials that await those bold enough to step into this fractured world.

In the bustling halls of Bandai Namco, a pivotal moment unfolded in November 2021 when the gaming giant unveiled the closed beta for a highly anticipated title. Players eagerly signed up, hoping to be among the first to test the immersive world that awaited them. The excitement was palpable as the full release of Elden Ring was initially set for January 21, 2022. However, the clock ticked on, and the date was shifted to March 15 of the same year, much to the chagrin of eager fans.

As the launch day arrived, a wave of players dove into the game, only to find that not all was smooth sailing. Performance issues plagued the experience, with many voicing their frustration over an insufficient frame rate. Bandai Namco, ever responsive to its community, set to work, rolling out software patches and updates to address these concerns.

In a move that captured the imagination of fans, a crowdfunding campaign for an Elden Ring-themed board game by Steamforged Games took flight on Kickstarter in November 2022, adding yet another layer to the growing Elden Ring universe. Then, in February 2023, excitement surged again with the announcement of an expansion titled Shadow of the Erdtree, promising to enrich the lore and experience further.

As the dust settled after the launch, Elden Ring emerged as a titan in the gaming sphere, earning "universal acclaim" on the review aggregator site Metacritic. Its debut on the video-sharing platform Twitch was nothing short of spectacular, drawing nearly 900,000 viewers within the first 24 hours, placing it third among the most popular launches, only trailing behind Lost Ark and Cyberpunk 2077.

Critics sang the praises of the game’s vast open-world setting, with Tamoor Hussain from GameSpot heralding the Lands Between as the most expansive of FromSoftware's creations. He emphasized that the heart of the game lay in its exploration and discovery. Similarly, Mitchell Saltzman of IGN lauded Elden Ring for its rewarding exploration, affirming that every corner of the map held the promise of adventure.

In the year 2022, a new saga began to unfold in the realm of gaming with the release of "Elden Ring," an action role-playing game crafted by the renowned developers at FromSoftware. Under the visionary direction of Hidetaka Miyazaki, and enriched by the imaginative worldbuilding of fantasy author George R. R. Martin, this game would soon captivate players across the globe. On March 15, it made its debut on multiple platforms, including PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Windows, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X/S, brought to life by FromSoftware in Japan and Bandai Namco Entertainment internationally.

As players stepped into this expansive universe, they found themselves embodying a customizable character on a noble quest: to restore the shattered Elden Ring and ascend as the new Elden Lord. The journey was experienced through a third-person perspective, allowing adventurers to roam freely across an intricately designed open world. The vastness of this land was divided into six main areas, each waiting to be explored atop the trusty steed, Torrent, which served as the primary means of travel.

Hidden dungeons, laid out in a linear fashion, beckoned with promises of valuable items for those daring enough to delve into their depths. The choices were plentiful, as players wielded an array of weapons and harnessed powerful magic spells, employing stealth mechanics for those who preferred a more subtle approach to their encounters. Scattered throughout this enchanting world were checkpoints that not only facilitated fast travel but also presented opportunities for players to enhance their abilities using the in-game currency known as Runes.

In addition to the solitary journey, "Elden Ring" offered an online multiplayer mode, inviting players to join forces for cooperative battles or engage in thrilling player-versus-player combat. In the early stages of development, FromSoftware aspired to create an open-world experience that echoed the beloved gameplay of the Dark Souls series, envisioning "Elden Ring" as a bold evolution of their iconic franchise. With admiration for Martin's storytelling prowess, Miyazaki hoped that his involvement would weave a more accessible narrative, one that would resonate deeply with both new and seasoned players alike. Thus, a grand adventure awaited, filled with challenges, camaraderie, and the promise of legend.

On a chilly day in February, specifically the 13th of 2022, excitement buzzed in the air as news spread about the upcoming release of Elden Ring. Set to arrive on March 10, 2022, this highly anticipated game would be available on multiple platforms including PS4, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, and PC. For those eager to embark on this adventure, pre-ordering Elden Ring on the Xbox One presented an enticing opportunity.

Miyazaki has said that Elden Ring will be “full of things that we weren’t able to do in the Dark Souls series.” The game will focus “more heavily on RPG elements,” Miyazaki said in an interview from 2019, and “will include a wide variety of weapons, magic, and ways to engage enemies, that make it possible to provide users with a style of gameplay and strategy that suits them.” FromSoftware fans have since been wracked with Elden Ring-information withdrawal, leading the community to fill the void with its own, self-created lore. Elden Ring is coming to PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Windows PC, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X next year. FromSoftware is finally on the cusp of releasing Elden Ring, the much-anticipated follow-up to Dark Souls, Bloodborne and Sekiro. Exploring the past isn’t a new concept for FromSoftware. The first and only Dark Souls DLC, Artorias of the Abyss, sent players hundreds of years into the past to confront Manus, Father of the Abyss. [28][27] As with many of FromSoftware's previous games, the story was designed to be poorly explained; the developers wanted players to interpret the story for themselves via flavor text and optional discussions with non-player characters (NPCs). He noted FromSoftware did not want a linear or story-focused game, and by giving Martin oversight of a backstory with which the player is not directly involved, he could allow Martin to freely design his contributions.[26] Some staff from Game of Thrones, a television series adaptation of A Song of Ice and Fire, also assisted with the game's development.[30]  Production of the game began in early 2017 following the release of The Ringed City, a piece of downloadable content (DLC) for Dark Souls III. Elden Ring was developed alongside Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice, which Miyazaki also directed. He said although the combat in Elden Ring has similarities to that in Sekiro, neither game directly inspired the mechanics of the other.[31][32] FromSoftware simultaneously develop both games using a "co-director" structure in which each of the games had a staff member acting as director through its first stages of development.

Elden Ring has a multiplayer system that allows players to be summoned for both cooperative and player-versus-player (PvP) play over the Internet.[19] Cooperative play involves the placing of a summon sign on the ground, which causes the sign to become visible to online players who have used a corresponding item. If another player interacts with the sign, the player who placed the sign is summoned into their world. Cooperative players remain in the same world until the boss of the area is defeated or until a summoned player dies and is returned to their home world. In PvP combat, a summon sign is used to challenge another player to a duel, or the player can use additional items to invade the worlds of others. World hosts may use a "taunter's tongue" to increase the likelihood their world will be invaded by others and to decrease the time between invasions.[20][21][22]  Elden Ring takes place in the Lands Between, a fictional realm over which several demigods rule. It was previously ruled over by the immortal Queen Marika, who acted as keeper of the Elden Ring, a powerful force that manifested itself as the physical concept of order. When Marika shattered the Elden Ring and disappeared, her demigod children began fighting over pieces of the Ring in an event called The Shattering. Each demigod has a shard of the Ring called a Great Rune, which corrupts them with power.

In the game, the player character is a Tarnished, one of a group of exiles from the Lands Between who are summoned back after the Shattering. The player must traverse the realm to repair the Elden Ring and become the Elden Lord.  Early in their journey to repair the Elden Ring, the player-character the Tarnished encounters a maiden named Melina. Because the Tarnished is maidenless, Melina offers to act as their maiden, granting them the ability to turn runes into strength and giving the Tarnished a steed named Torrent. In exchange, Melina requires the Tarnished to take her to the Erdtree, the home of the Elden Ring. Melina later takes the Tarnished to the Roundtable Hold, a gathering place for other Tarnished seeking to repair the Elden Ring. The Hold's benefactor the Two Fingers instructs the Tarnished to collect the Great Runes and bring them to the Erdtree, where they can be used to repair the Elden Ring.  The Tarnished travels into the Lands Between, investigating its locales and defeating the demigods. After recovering at least two Great Runes, the Two Fingers allows them to battle Morgott the Grace-Given, the demigod guarding the Erdtree. The Tarnished defeats Morgott but find a wall of thorns blocking the Edtree's interior.

FromSoftware's Dark Souls series of games is noted for its high level of difficulty.[23][24] Director Hidetaka Miyazaki wanted to create an open-world game, intending Elden Ring as a mechanical evolution of Dark Souls. Elden Ring was designed to have a more-expansive environment than the narrow dungeons of FromSoftware's previous games; Miyazaki hoped the grander scale would add freedom and depth to exploration. FromSoftware approached American author George R. R. Martin, creator of the fantasy-novel series A Song of Ice and Fire, to provide worldbuilding for Elden Ring. Miyazaki, who is a fan of Martin's work, hoped Martin's contributions would produce a more-accessible narrative than those the studio's earlier games.[25][26][27]  Miyazaki remained lead writer for the game's main storyline but gave Martin creative freedom to write about events that occurred before the main narrative. Miyazaki compared the process to using a "dungeon master's handbook in a tabletop RPG".

As per the director, the game has been made so that players will find it “very difficult, but it can be handled.” Don’t expect difficulty settings, then, or any hand-holding throughout the entire experience. Veteran Soulsbourne players, eat your hearts out.  Miyazaki explains that he feels “the difficulty level as a pure action game is lower.” Instead, Miyazaki states “I think that the closest image of difficulty is Dark Souls III.

query: Who did Iga Swiatek defeat to win the Qatar Open 2022?
positive:
Once upon a time in the vibrant city of Doha, Iga Swiatek embarked on a remarkable journey that led her to claim her first title of the season. Along the way, she faced off against the top ten players, triumphing against formidable opponents. This victory marked a significant milestone for her, as it was her second WTA 1000 title. However, the road to glory had not been without its challenges. Before arriving in Doha, Swiatek had yet to secure a win against Maria Sakkari, who had bested her three times, and Aryna Sabalenka, who had claimed victory once. But in a stunning turn of events, Swiatek defeated both players in back-to-back matches, and since that moment, she has not tasted defeat against either.

In her quest for the title, Swiatek faced Viktorija Golubic in the first round, where she experienced a rare setback, losing just one set. Yet, she would not let that deter her. In the final, she squared off against Garbiñe Muguruza, losing only two games and demonstrating her dominance on the court. The year 2022 proved to be a defining one for Swiatek, as she amassed an impressive twenty wins, ultimately defeating Sakkari 6-4, 6-1 in the final, the most victories on tour that year. Following her triumph, she soared to a new career-high ranking, a testament to her hard work and perseverance.

As the season unfolded, Swiatek showcased her resilience, coming back from a set down in Indian Wells against Anhelina Kalinina, Clara Tauson, and Angelique Kerber in her first three matches. In total, she achieved seven comebacks in 2022, a remarkable feat considering she had only managed eight in her entire career prior to this season. Her remarkable streak continued with eleven consecutive wins, a testament to her tenacity and skill, leaving her opponents in awe as she solidified her place among the sport's elite.

In January 2022, a remarkable journey began for Iga Świątek as she achieved an impressive milestone: reaching the second week of a Grand Slam at six consecutive majors. This streak commenced with her triumphant victory at the 2020 French Open, setting a new record for the longest stretch of advancing to the second week. Her path was not without challenges; in the quarterfinals, she faced the formidable Kaia Kanepi, battling through a grueling match that lasted three hours and one minute. Unfortunately, the following day, she fell to Danielle Collins in the semifinal.

As February rolled around, Świątek entered the Qatar Open, where she was seeded seventh. With determination, she dispatched Viktorija Golubic and Daria Kasatkina in the second and third rounds, respectively. The quarterfinals brought her face to face with the top-seeded Aryna Sabalenka, a match she won, propelling her into the semifinals against Maria Sakkari. In a display of skill and composure, she emerged victorious in straight sets. The final showcased her prowess as she defeated the fifth-seeded Elina Svitolina, losing only two games, and thus claimed her second WTA 1000 championship title.

The excitement continued at Indian Wells, where Świątek reached her third WTA 1000 final. As the third seed, she showcased her talent by overcoming Anhelina Kalinina, Clara Tauson, and Angelique Kerber in three sets, followed by straight-set victories over Madison Keys and Simona Halep. In a stunning display, she bested the sixth seed Maria Sakkari in the final, clinching her fifth title. These back-to-back Masters victories catapulted Świątek to a career-high ranking of world No. 2, marking a significant chapter in her burgeoning tennis career.

In March 2021, a significant milestone was reached as she cracked the top 15 for the very first time in her career. Seeded at 15, Świątek made waves at the Italian Open, claiming her inaugural WTA 1000 title in a stunning display against the former champion, Karolína Plíšková, in a mere 46 minutes, and remarkably, without losing a single game. Her path to the final was nothing short of extraordinary, as she overcame two-time champion and world No. 5, Elina Svitolina, along with the highly-ranked teenager, Coco Gauff, all on the same day. In a thrilling third-round match against Barbora Krejčíková, she even saved match points, showcasing her resilience and determination. This victory placed her among an elite group, becoming the third player in 2021 to secure a title after saving match points, following in the footsteps of Naomi Osaka at the Australian Open and Ashleigh Barty in Miami. Additionally, she etched her name in history as the fourth teenager to win a WTA 1000 event. With this triumphant run to her third career title, she ascended into the top 10 of the singles rankings, standing proud as world No. 9 on May 17, 2021. As the French Open approached, Świątek was seeded at No. 8. She embarked on her title defense with a victory against Kaja Juvan in the first round, before continuing her quest by defeating Rebecca Peterson, Aryna Sabalenka, and Marta Kostyuk.
negative: The defending champion needed just 53 minutes to advance, 6-0, 6-1, on Wednesday at the Qatar TotalEnergies Open.ByTENNIS.comPublished Feb 15, 2023 copy_link Published Feb 15, 2023 Iga Swiatek returned to the site where her masterful rise in 2022 began with a 37-match win streak. And what a statement the world No. 1 made at the Qatar TotalEnergies Open in Doha Wednesday.On paper, Swiatek’s opening match presented intrigue. Across the net was Danielle Collins, one of the nine players to defeat the Pole during a banner 2022 season that saw her win eight titles and completely own her status as the 28th world No. 1 in WTA history.In reality, today's show was a solo act. Swiatek completely silenced Collins, serving up another bakery special with a 6-0, 6-1 victory."I'm happy that I was kind of composed and from the beginning till the end pretty focused and disciplined with tactics. So I didn't really let Danielle get into the rhythm," Swiatek told press. "I wanted to be aggressive. I'm pretty happy that I did that well." On paper, Swiatek’s opening match presented intrigue.

Iga Swiatek has done it again. For the second time this year, she’s standing at the top of a podium, holding a Grand Slam trophy above her head. This time, she defeated Ons Jabeur 6-2, 7-6(5) to win the 2022 US Open, capping off a year of incredible and unexpected success. Swiatek, who looked extremely nervous before the match began, looked anything but once the rackets started swinging. She came out strong, showing off all the skills we know her for: explosive movement, pinpoint precision and a soft touch. By comparison, Jabeur, who looked comfortable and happy before the match started, came out looking flat and tentative. It wasn't until Swiatek was up 3-0 that Jabeur got on the board. Then, in the fifth game of the first set, Jabeur hit four winners, each one perfectly placed along the singles sideline. All Swiatek could do with those is watch them bounce as Jabeur narrowed the gap at 3-2. Unfortunately, that was the last game Jabeur won in the first set. Swiatek was able to reach balls that most people couldn't even try for, while Jabeur couldn't stop hitting the ball into the net at the most vital moments. Swiatek claimed the first set in 30 minutes flat, and made it look as effortless as ever.

The second set was entirely different from the first. While Jabeur again didn't win a game until Swiatek was up 3-0, she finally found her gear. Her excellent slice finally began to appear and she began going to the net more, helping her win four of the next five games. Swiatek appeared to have finally wrestled the momentum of the match back to her side, breaking a 5-5 tie to get within one game of the title, but she couldn't close and Jabeur tied it up once again. The tiebreak was close, and it looked like Jabeur could run away with it at any moment, but an unforced error from Jabeur ended the tiebreak and gave Swiatek her first US Open championship and her third career Grand Slam trophy. When it was all over, Swiatek and Jabeur shared a heartfelt embrace, and then Swiatek immediately burst into happy tears. She'd earned it. This victory puts an exclamation point on the best year of Swiatek's young career — or the best year of anyone’s career, really. She started 2022 by falling just short of the finals at the Australian Open (she was knocked out in the semis by Danielle Collins), but she rebounded to produce one of the most dominant stretches of tennis we’ve seen in decades. Swiatek beat Annet Kontaveit to win the Qatar Open.

query: Which country won the most medals at the 2022 Winter Olympics?
